title: Traffic Systems Skills

content.statement: Every service business can be diagnosed by examining whether traffic, systems, or skills are limiting growth.
content.question: Is your current business bottleneck traffic, systems, or skills?
content.mental_model: Three-legged stool, if one leg is weak, the whole business wobbles.
content.business_lens: Use this to find whether the business needs more leads, better sales/operations, or stronger fulfillment.
content.life_lens: Use this to identify whether your problem is access, structure, or ability.
sop: Rate traffic from low to high (see calculation, page 6)
Rate systems from low to high (see calculation, page 6)
Rate skills from low to high (see calculation, page 6)
Fix that area before optimizing the others
content.explainer: Traffic means the number of qualified people discovering your offer. Systems are the repeatable processes that turn attention into money, contracts, delivery, follow-up, and retention. Skills are the actual ability to produce the promised result. A business with strong skills but no traffic stays invisible. A business with traffic but weak systems loses opportunities. A business with sales systems but weak skills creates disappointed clients. This framework gives you a simple way to stop guessing and start diagnosing.
questions: Is your current business bottleneck traffic, systems, or skills?
If you fixed your weakest of the three today, which of the other two would still quietly cap your growth?
What's a time you added more traffic to a business that couldn't actually convert or deliver on what it already had?

definitions.def: The slowest or most constrained step in a system.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Traffic 8, Systems 3, Skills 9. Business Strength = 8 times 3 times 9 = 216. Now imagine all three at 7: 7 times 7 times 7 equals 343, higher, because the multiplication punishes the weak link. Higher combined score means growth compounds cleanly; one very low factor (like Systems at 3) drags the whole score down even if the other two are strong.
content.closing_statement: The weakest leg sets the ceiling for the rest.

caption:
Principle Nº 245: Traffic Systems Skills

Every service business can be diagnosed by examining whether traffic, systems, or skills are limiting growth. Traffic means the number of qualified people discovering your offer.

Ask yourself: Is your current business bottleneck traffic, systems, or skills?
